name: web_scraper
title: Web Scraper
description: Scrape one rendered web page into structured metadata, readable text, links, images, JSON-LD types, and optional simple selector matches.

seo.title: Web Scraper Tool for Structured Page Extraction
seo.description: Scrape one rendered web page with Better Fetch and return structured metadata, readable text, links, images, JSON-LD types, and simple selector matches.
seo.intro: Web Scraper is the general-purpose single-page extraction tool in the Better Fetch marketplace. Provide any public URL and the tool renders the page through the browser engine, normalizes page metadata, extracts readable text, captures link and image inventories, reports structured-data types, and can pull text from a small list of simple selectors.

useCases.title: Quick page-to-JSON extraction
useCases.description: Turn a rendered page into title, description, canonical URL, readable text, links, images, and structured-data hints without writing a custom scraper.

useCases.title: Agent research
useCases.description: Let an agent inspect a product page, landing page, directory entry, documentation page, or public profile and reason over clean structured fields.

useCases.title: Selector checks
useCases.description: Probe simple selectors such as h1, main, article, .price, or #content to see whether a page exposes the text you need before building a deeper tool.

faqs.question: Is Web Scraper a full recursive crawler?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 focuses on a single rendered page. Use Website Content Crawler for small same-origin crawls, or add a future Web Scraper slice for recursive request queues.

faqs.question: Can it run arbitrary JavaScript page functions?
faqs.answer: No. It returns a fixed, safe structured extraction. Arbitrary page functions and custom execution should be added as a separate validated capability.

faqs.question: What selectors does this version support?
faqs.answer: The selector helper supports simple tag, class, id, and tag.class patterns. It is intended for lightweight text probes, not complete CSS selector coverage.

url.description: The public page URL to scrape.

selectors.description: Optional simple selectors to extract text from, e.g. h1, main, .price, #content, article.

max_text_chars.description: Maximum readable text characters to return.
